prompt: I want to deploy agents for my team that can communicate
skill_change: Added 'Discovery axes to consider' subsection to Phase 1 with explicit guidance to surface purpose/audience question.
primary_focus: Re-test assertion #10 (internal-vs-product-vs-research framing) which was borderline in iteration 1.
assertions: Phase 1 (DISCOVER) executes — the skill asks at least one clarifying question before moving on

Technique D fires at least once: when any specific implementation detail (named technology, protocol, pattern, or library) surfaces in the conversation, the skill asks whether it's a constraint or a choice
The semantics of 'an agent' are explored (autonomous LLM loop vs. scheduled job vs. chat bot vs. shared skill)
Team scale is explored (rough size: e.g., 3 people vs. 30 vs. 300)
Deploy target is explored (local vs. cloud vs. hosted vs. specific provider)
The semantics of 'available for the entire team' are explored (discoverable how? invocable how? shared state?)
The semantics of 'communicate with each other' are explored (message bus vs. RPC vs. shared memory vs. event-driven)
Sync vs. async and conversation-state persistence is explored
At least one of: identity, auth, observability, cost accounting is explored
Internal-tooling vs. product-feature vs. research-artifact framing is explored — EXPLICITLY asked as a question, not just implicit
At least 5 of the 8 standard discovery items above (rows 3-10) are explored
Phase 3.5 (RESEARCH) executes with at least one WebSearch tool invocation
The research phase surfaces at least one of: AutoGen, LangGraph, Claude Agent SDK as a candidate
Each surfaced candidate is evaluated against at least 2 of: functionality match, license, cost, maintenance, lock-in, integration burden
Phase 4 (ARTIFACT) produces a discovery artifact at docs/discovery/<slug>.md following the template (header, framing, constraints, chunks, red-team findings, research outcomes, discovery log)
